title: Choose MeshCore app message codec and schema integration strategy
description: Why this exists: MeshCore app messages may not share Meshtastic protobuf framing, and Zephyr needs a bounded, maintainable codec. What needs to be done: identify the message format from research, choose a bounded decode/encode strategy suitable for Zephyr C, decide whether generated code, handwritten parsers, or shared protocol headers are appropriate, and document schema/source provenance. Acceptance criteria: codec strategy builds for native_sim and representative hardware targets when implemented; malformed/oversized input handling is specified; no unbounded allocation assumptions; source version/provenance is recorded.
design:
MeshCore app codec/schema strategy, recorded 2026-07-01.

Source/provenance baseline:
- meshcore-dev/MeshCore e8d3c53ba1ea863937081cd0caad759b832f3028: docs/companion_protocol.md; examples/companion_radio/MyMesh.cpp/MyMesh.h; src/helpers/BaseSerialInterface.h; src/helpers/ArduinoSerialInterface.cpp; src/helpers/nrf52/SerialBLEInterface.cpp; src/helpers/esp32/SerialBLEInterface.cpp; src/helpers/esp32/SerialWifiInterface.cpp.
- meshcore-dev/meshcore-open e1b8d6ec97d6ccca0eee8122864092f3af3f2062: documentation/ble-protocol.md; lib/connector/meshcore_protocol.dart; lib/connector/meshcore_connector.dart; lib/services/usb_serial_frame_codec.dart.
- meshcore-dev/meshcore_py 5bac3573b51c4298062881885b6d15a994109076 and meshcore-dev/meshcore.js 306dadac1cdacfd071ab6899e5c84e7def7c4425 are secondary client references, not the authoritative schema.
- The protocol is not protobuf, CBOR, JSON, nanopb, zcbor, or LICHEN Native. It is a fixed byte-command companion API: one command/response byte followed by command-specific little-endian fields and bounded strings/bytes. Serial/TCP add only a 3-byte outer frame.

Decision:
- Use a handwritten Zephyr C codec/dispatcher helper library for MeshCore compatibility.
- Do not generate code from MeshCore sources for the MVP. The upstream protocol is defined in C/C++ constants and prose/client code, not a machine-readable schema. A generator would add fragile scraping without improving safety.
- Keep generated artifacts out of the firmware path. If helper scripts are added later, use them only to refresh test vectors/tables from pinned upstream sources.
- Put protocol constants in a project-owned header with the pinned source SHA string, similar to LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_PROTOBUF_COMMIT. Example names: LICHEN_MESHCORE_SOURCE_MESHCORE_COMMIT, LICHEN_MESHCORE_APP_PROTOCOL_VERSION, LICHEN_MESHCORE_FRAME_MAX, LICHEN_MESHCORE_SERIAL_PAYLOAD_MAX.

Recommended module shape:
- New subsystem directory: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshcore/.
- Kconfig symbols:
  - LICHEN_MESHCORE_CODEC: bounded app-compat byte codec.
  - LICHEN_MESHCORE_ADAPTER: dispatcher/session layer on top of codec.
  - LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_FRAME: default 176 to match firmware MAX_FRAME_SIZE, with stricter transport limits enforced where needed.
  - LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_SERIAL_PAYLOAD: default 172 for client-facing serial/TCP payload expectations.
  - LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_TEXT: default 160 for app text payloads.
- Public headers under include/lichen/meshcore/: codec.h and adapter.h.
- codec.c should own byte-level parsing/encoding only; adapter.c should own command dispatch, queues, response policy, local-info callbacks, and session state.

Codec API shape:
- Decode functions should return typed views into the caller-owned input buffer, not heap-allocated copies.
- Encode functions should write to caller-provided buffers and return positive encoded length or negative errno.
- Suggested top-level types:
  - enum lichen_meshcore_command_id and enum lichen_meshcore_response_id for known byte values.
  - struct lichen_meshcore_frame_view { uint8_t type; const uint8_t *payload; size_t payload_len; }.
  - struct lichen_meshcore_channel_text, direct_text, device_query, get_channel, channel_info, contact_record, self_info, device_info, battery_storage, error, queued_message.
- Use helpers for read/write little-endian u16/u32/i32 and fixed/variable strings. Helpers must check remaining length before every read/write.
- Keep unknown commands as raw frame_view so the adapter can produce deterministic ERR_UNSUPPORTED rather than the codec hiding them.

Transport framing:
- BLE NUS receives/emits one raw MeshCore inner frame per GATT value. No length prefix.
- Serial/TCP framing is separate from the inner codec: RX app-to-device 0x3c + uint16_le payload_len + payload; TX device-to-app 0x3e + uint16_le payload_len + payload.
- Serial/TCP decoder should be a small bounded state machine that skips junk until start byte, rejects payload_len > configured max, waits for full payload, and then passes the payload to the inner codec. Do not merge serial framing with BLE raw payload handling.

Malformed/oversized handling:
- NULL pointers, zero-length inner frames, truncated fixed fields, string/payload lengths beyond remaining data, invalid channel indexes, and invalid path lengths return -EINVAL from codec helpers.
- Input payloads above LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_FRAME return -EMSGSIZE before command-specific parsing.
- Serial/TCP payload_len above LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_SERIAL_PAYLOAD returns -EMSGSIZE and resynchronizes at the next start marker.
- Unsupported known or unknown command bytes are not codec errors once the frame shape is valid; they decode as raw command IDs so the adapter can return MeshCore RESP_CODE_ERR/ERR_CODE_UNSUPPORTED_CMD.
- Encoders return -ENOBUFS if the caller buffer cannot fit the complete response. They must not partially publish frames.
- All parsing/encoding must be no-allocation and suitable for native_sim, qemu, nRF52840, ESP32-S3, RP2040, and STM32WL-class constraints.

Minimum MVP encode/decode coverage:
- Decode: APP_START, DEVICE_QUERY, GET_CONTACTS, GET_CHANNEL, SYNC_NEXT_MESSAGE, GET_BATT_AND_STORAGE, GET/SET_DEVICE_TIME, SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG, SEND_TXT_MSG/direct, SEND_CHANNEL_DATA as optional/raw, SET_CHANNEL as policy-gated, and unknown/unsupported command handling.
- Encode: OK, ERR with MeshCore error byte, SELF_INFO, DEVICE_INFO, CONTACTS_START, CONTACT, CONTACT_END, CHANNEL_INFO, NO_MORE_MESSAGES, BATTERY/STORAGE, CURRENT_TIME, MSG_SENT or OK for channel-send drift cases, MSG_WAITING push, CHANNEL_MSG_RECV/V3, CONTACT_MSG_RECV/V3, CHANNEL_DATA_RECV optional.
- Exact command/response classification remains in project-LICHEN-9c4y.13; drift cases remain in project-LICHEN-9c4y.16.

Test/vector strategy:
- Add canonical JSON vectors under test/vectors/meshcore_app_compat.json or similar. Include source commit SHAs, frame hex, direction, transport kind where relevant, parsed fields, expected response/error, and notes for drift cases.
- Add a native_sim ztest under lichen/tests/meshcore_codec/ covering canonical vectors, little-endian fields, serial/TCP framing, BLE raw frames, boundary lengths, malformed/truncated frames, oversized frames, unknown commands, no-allocation behavior, and response encoding.
- Use direct byte-for-byte expected outputs for all encoded responses that represent packet formats. This follows the project rule that every packet format has test vectors.
- Add at least one cross-source drift vector for CMD_SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG ACK behavior and mark the selected target-client behavior once project-LICHEN-9c4y.16 resolves it.

Build strategy when implemented:
- First build and run meshcore_codec on native_sim locally/EC2: west build -b native_sim lichen/tests/meshcore_codec && west build -t run.
- Run Twister for meshcore_codec on native_sim through the EC2 Zephyr cache.
- Build representative no-hardware board configs where feasible with the codec enabled but no BLE hardware dependency: native_sim, qemu_x86 if configured, and at least one board-agnostic gateway/native_sim overlay.
- The codec itself must not depend on BT, sockets, LoRa devices, or gateway app internals; only adapter/transport layers may select those dependencies.

Downstream notes:
- project-LICHEN-9c4y.5 should implement the serial/TCP frame state machine and dispatcher using this codec split.
- project-LICHEN-9c4y.6/.7/.8 should use typed codec structs and adapter callbacks, not parse bytes directly in handlers.
- project-LICHEN-9c4y.10 should include meshcore_codec in its native_sim/Twister harness once the codec exists.
notes: Review evidence: two independent review subagents completed after the codec strategy and vector follow-up were recorded. Ptolemy the 2nd found no P0/P1/P2 blockers and confirmed message format, bounded handwritten C strategy, generated-vs-handwritten decision, malformed/oversized handling, no-allocation constraints, source provenance, and follow-up coverage. Huygens the 2nd found no P0/P1/P2 blockers and cross-checked MeshCore raw byte frames, BLE raw framing, serial/TCP 0x3c/0x3e uint16_le framing, 176-vs-172 size distinction, and Meshtastic codec style. Residual risks are tracked by project-LICHEN-9c4y.16 and project-LICHEN-9c4y.18.
close_reason: Completed: codec/schema strategy recorded with source provenance, bounded no-allocation C approach, malformed handling, vector/test plan, and two clean reviews.
